column1: SwissHacks 2026 — Portfolios & Transaction History
What's in this workbook
Portfolio Strategies          CIO sub-asset-class targets (weights + CHF) per strategy.
CIO Recommendation List       The bank's conviction list: BUY / HOLD / SELL ratings for held
instruments plus BUY-rated swap candidates not yet held.
Sample Portfolio Defensive    Positions for the Defensive mandate (Target + Current values).
Sample Portfolio Balanced     Positions for the Balanced mandate (Target + Current values).
Sample Portfolio Growth       Positions for the Growth mandate (Target + Current values).
Transactions Defensive        3-year trade history for the Defensive portfolio.
Transactions Balanced         3-year trade history for the Balanced portfolio.
Transactions Growth           3-year trade history for the Growth portfolio.
Cash Flows                    Deposits, withdrawals, and management fees per portfolio.
Conventions
• All CHF amounts are in Swiss Francs.
• ISINs follow ISO 6166 (12 chars). Cash positions use the placeholder Cash-XXX.
• Equity prices come from real historical closes; bonds are priced at par (100% face).
• For bonds, Quantity = face value ÷ 100, so Quantity × Price (local) = face value.
• For equities, Quantity = number of shares.
• Summing BUY − SELL quantities for an ISIN gives the current position; multiplied by
the current price (in CHF) it matches Current (CHF) on the Sample Portfolio sheet.
Targets, current values, and mandate drift
Each portfolio was rebalanced to exactly CHF 10,000,000 in a rebalance window in
late April 2026, completed 2026-05-02 (the final 'precision rebalance' trades at the
end of each Transactions sheet). Target (CHF) is that rebalance allocation per
position — it still sums to CHF 10,000,000.
Current (CHF) is the position's market value today: prices have moved since the
rebalance, so portfolio totals no longer equal CHF 10M and sub-asset-class weights
have drifted off their Portfolio Strategies targets.
Market since the rebalance: a tech-led rally — global tech up strongly, broad
developed-market equities up moderately, financials lagging, EM equities slightly
down (FX), bonds roughly flat, gold up. The same ISIN moved identically everywhere;
portfolios drift differently only because their allocations differ.
THE BANK'S DRIFT RULE: a sub-asset class may deviate at most ±2.0 percentage points
from its target weight (recomputed against the drifted total). Beyond that, the RM
must propose a rebalance — detecting such breaches is part of your mission.
Looking up instruments in market-data providers
Each Sample Portfolio sheet has three extra columns to save you a search:
• Valor          SIX Financial Data unique numeric identifier for the instrument.
MIC            Market Identifier Code of the most-liquid listing.
Combine as f"{Valor}_{MIC}" for SIX MCP listing tools
(end_of_day_snapshot, intraday_snapshot, end_of_day_history).
Use Valor alone for instrument tools (instrument_base, etc.).
• Yahoo Ticker   Ticker on Yahoo Finance for the most-liquid listing.
Use directly with yfinance / Yahoo Finance API.
Notes:
• Bonds and cash positions have blank Valor and Yahoo Ticker — bonds vary too
much by ISIN to index by issuer name; for bonds use SIX `instrument_symbology`
with the ISIN directly.
• A few specialty funds (UBS Real Estate Interswiss, Partners Group LIP) have no
Yahoo coverage and no clean SIX match — both columns will be blank.
SIX coverage
Every ISIN in this workbook — portfolio holdings, bonds, and CIO recommendation-list
candidates — resolves directly in SIX MCP by ISIN (instruments(ids, scheme: ISIN)).
The Valor and MIC columns are that resolution, precomputed for you.
Caveat: resolution covers IDENTITY (reference data, symbology). Some bonds list only
on thin venues (ETLX, FINR, OTCM, …) where end-of-day price HISTORY may be sparse —
for bond prices, par-pricing (as used in the transaction sheets) is the safe fallback.
CIO Recommendation List columns
Rating           BUY (CIO preferred) · HOLD (keep, no new money) · SELL (replace).
Rating Since     Date the current rating took effect. A days-old SELL on a position
still held tells you how long the gap has gone unactioned.
CIO View         One-line rationale behind the rating.
As Of            Publication date of this list (same for every row).
Notes:
• Mandate compliance is enforced at sub-asset-class weights (Portfolio Strategies);
within a slot, selection is constrained by CIO rating and the client's personal context.
• A SELL rating on a held position means the asset left the recommended list but the
quarterly review hasn't actioned it yet — exactly the alert your dashboard should raise.
• Two legacy cantonal-bank holdings are deliberately NOT on the list: CIO research
does not cover them. Absence is itself a signal.
• Every SELL has at least one not-held BUY candidate in the same Industry Group.
Transaction columns
Transaction ID   Unique row identifier (not chronologically ordered — sort by Timestamp).
Timestamp        Date of the trade or income event.
Portfolio        Defensive · Balanced · Growth
ISIN             Security identifier.
Issuer / Asset   Human-readable issuer name.
Side             BUY · SELL
Quantity         Shares (equities/ETFs) or face value ÷ 100 (bonds).
Price (local)    Price per unit in the security's native currency.
Currency         Native currency (CHF, USD, EUR, GBP, …).
FX → CHF         Native-to-CHF FX rate on the transaction date.
Price (CHF)      = Price (local) × FX → CHF.
Amount (CHF)     = Quantity × Price (CHF).
Rationale        Short human-readable reason for the trade.
Price Source     yfinance · synth_estimated · issuance_par · cash
Cash Flow columns
Flow ID          Unique row identifier.
Timestamp        Date of the cash event.
Portfolio        Defensive · Balanced · Growth
Side             DEPOSIT · WITHDRAWAL · FEE · COUPON
Amount (CHF)     Absolute CHF amount of the flow.
Rationale        Short human-readable description.
